subject: Time Machine backup failed with sparse error message
description: Time Machine reports backup could not complete; user needs data protected before travel.

ts_steps.title: Capture error text
ts_steps.action: Time Machine settings → note exact failure message and time.

ts_steps.title: Free space on destination
ts_steps.action: Finder: confirm backup volume free space versus next backup size; NAS sparsebundle needs headroom.

ts_steps.title: First Aid internal disk
ts_steps.action: Disk Utility → Macintosh HD Data → First Aid — fix internal errors before trusting new backups.

ts_steps.title: First Aid backup volume
ts_steps.action: If direct-attached backup disk: First Aid on that volume. Network: use NAS admin tools instead.

ts_steps.title: Thin with exclusions
ts_steps.action: Time Machine → Options — exclude VMs, large caches, Docker images; run backup again.

ts_steps.title: Local snapshots
ts_steps.action: Optional Terminal: tmutil listlocalsnapshots / — thin with IT approval if bloated.

ts_steps.title: New backup destination
ts_steps.action: Add fresh APFS volume or new SMB share; point Time Machine; take full backup; verify.

ts_steps.title: NAS permissions
ts_steps.action: Confirm SMB/AFP settings per Apple Time Machine on NAS KB; user ACL read/write.

ts_steps.title: Sparsebundle corruption
ts_steps.action: If bundle corrupt and IT approves, archive old bundle read-only and start new bundle name on same NAS.

internal_information: Full disk, corrupt bundle, permissions. New destination often fastest recovery.
